name: Cala Morell Necropolis
description: A cave necropolis of fourteen hypogea carved into the rock, in use from pre-Talayotic times to the second century AD. Some chambers feature interior columns, windows, and a façade with relief columns of classical influence.

highlights: the necropolis of 14 caves cut into the rock (hypogea)
the interior details: columns, windows and reliefs
combining it with a swim in the cove at Cala Morell
